sections.heading: Cluster Configuration Options
sections.body:
Each Atlas cluster tier comes with a default set of resources. Atlas
provides the following resource configuration options:Custom Storage SizeThe size of the server root volume. Atlas clusters deployed onto
AWS (Amazon Web Services) use general purpose SSD (Solid State Disk)s [^amazon-disk-docs].> **Note:** 
>
> RAM Availability
>
> The actual amount of RAM available to each cluster tier might be
> slightly less than the stated amount, due to memory that the kernel
> reserves.The following cluster tiers are available:| Instance Size | Storage Range   | Default Storage | Default RAM |
| ------------- | --------------- | --------------- | ----------- |
| M0            | .5 GB           | .5 GB           | Shared      |
| Flex          | 5 GB            | 5 GB            | Flex        |
| M10           | 10 GB to 128 GB | 10 GB           | 2 GB        |
| M20           | 10 GB to 255 GB | 20 GB           | 4 GB        |
| M30           | 10 GB to 512 GB | 40 GB           | 8 GB        |
| M40           | 10 GB to 1 TB   | 80 GB           | 16 GB       |
| M40 Low-CPU   | 10 GB to 1 TB   | 80 GB           | 16 GB       |
| M40\_NVME     | 380 GB          | 380 GB          | 15.25 GB    |
| M50           | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 160 GB          | 32 GB       |
| M50 Low-CPU   | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 160 GB          | 32 GB       |
| M50\_NVME     | 760 GB          | 760 GB          | 30.5 GB     |
| M60           | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 320 GB          | 64 GB       |
| M60 Low-CPU   | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 320 GB          | 64 GB       |
| M60\_NVME     | 1.6 TB          | 1.6 TB          | 61 GB       |
| M80           | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 750 GB          | 131 GB      |
| M80 Low-CPU   | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 750 GB          | 122 GB      |
| M80\_NVME     | 1.6 TB          | 1.6 TB          | 122 GB      |
| M140          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 1 TB            | 192 GB      |
| M200          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 1.5 TB          | 256 GB      |
| M200 Low-CPU  | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 1.5 TB          | 256 GB      |
| M200\_NVME    | 3.1 TB          | 3.1 TB          | 244 GB      |
| M300          | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 2 TB            | 384 GB      |
| M300 Low-CPU  | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 2 TB            | 384 GB      |
| M400 Low-CPU  | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 3 TB            | 488 GB      |
| M400\_NVME    | 4 TB            | 4 TB            | 512 GB      |
| M700 Low-CPU  | 10 GB to 4 TB   | 4 TB            | 768 GB      | Can use this tier for a multi-cloud cluster. Unavailable in the **AP\_SOUTHEAST\_2** region. Unavailable in the **AP\_SOUTHEAST\_6** and **AP\_EAST\_2** regions.> **Note:** 
>
> Cluster Tier & API Naming Conventions
>
> For purposes of management with the [Atlas Administration API](atlas-api), cluster tier names that are prepended with `R`
> instead of an `M` (`R40` for example) run a [class](storage-class-ui) version of the cluster. When
> [creating](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupcluster) or
> [modifying](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcluster) a
> cluster with the API, be sure to specify your desired cluster class
> by name with the
> `replicationSpecs[n].regionConfigs[m].electableSpecs.instanceSize`
> attribute.> **Important:** 
>
> Multi-Cloud Low-CPU clusters
>
> Low-CPU cluster tiers (M40 Low-CPU, M50 Low-CPU, M60 Low-CPU, and so
> on) are available in [Configure High Availability and Workload Isolation](create-cluster-multi-region) as long as the cluster tier is
> available for all the regions that the cluster uses.Workloads typically require less than `2TB`.Custom Storage SpeedThe input/output operations per second (IOPS) the system can perform.Each cluster has a default IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) rate. You can also choose to
provision your tier's IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) rate to meet your particular needs.The selected cluster tier and custom storage size dictate the maximum
IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) for each storage speed.Encrypted Storage VolumesEncrypts the root volume for data at rest inside the volume and all data
moving between the volume and the cluster. Atlas uses
[Amazon EBS encryption](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSEncryption.html).

sections.heading: Amazon Availability Zones
sections.body:
Each AWS (Amazon Web Services) region includes a set number of
independent availability zones. Availability Zones consist of one or more
discrete data centers, each with redundant power, networking and connectivity,
housed in separate facilities. For regions that have at least three
availability zones (3AZ), Atlas deploys clusters across three availability
zones. For regions that only have two availability zones (2AZ), Atlas
deploys clusters across two availability zones.

The Atlas Add New Cluster form marks regions
that support at least three availability zones as **Recommended**, as
they provide higher availability.

The number of availability zones in a region has no effect on the number of MongoDB
nodes Atlas can deploy. MongoDB Atlas clusters are always made of
replica sets with a minimum of three MongoDB nodes.

For more information on the number of availability zones in a
given region, see the Amazon documentation on [global infrastructure](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/global-infrastructure/).

For more information on AWS (Amazon Web Services) regions and
availability zones, see the Amazon documentation on [using regions and
availability zones](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-regions-availability-zones.html)

sections.heading: Regions with at Least Three Availability Zones
sections.body:
Atlas clusters deployed in regions with at least three availability zones
are split across three availability zones. For example, a three node replica
set cluster would have one node deployed onto each availability zone.

![A 3-node replica set deployed across a 3-availability zone AWS region.](/reference/images/amazon-aws-3az.svg)

3AZ (Three Availability Zone) clusters have higher availability
compared to 2AZ (Two Availability Zone) clusters. However, not all
regions support 3AZ (Three Availability Zone) clusters.

sections.heading: Regions with Only Two Availability Zones
sections.body:
Atlas clusters deployed in regions with two availability zones are split
across the two availability zones. For example, a three node replica set
cluster would have two nodes deployed to one availability zone and the
remaining node deployed to the other availability zone.

![A 3-node replica set deployed across a 2-availability zone AWS region.](/reference/images/amazon-aws-2az.svg)

2AZ (Two Availability Zone) clusters have a higher chance of loss of
availability in the event of the loss of an availability zone than 3AZ
(Three Availability Zone) clusters. However, where latency or location are a
priority, a region that supports 2AZ (Two Availability Zone) clusters
may be preferred.

[^amazon-disk-docs]: For detailed documentation on Amazon storage options, see
    [Amazon EBS Volume Types](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSVolumeTypes.html)

sections.heading: Supported Regions
sections.body:
Atlas supports deploying separate [Search Nodes](configure-search-nodes) for `M10` and higher
clusters in the following AWS (Amazon Web Services) regions:**Americas:**Americas| AWS Region   | Location                 | Atlas Region   |
| ------------ | ------------------------ | -------------- |
| us-east-1    | Northern Virginia, USA   | US\_EAST\_1    |
| us-west-2    | Oregon, USA              | US\_WEST\_2    |
| ca-central-1 | Montreal, QC, Canada     | CA\_CENTRAL\_1 |
| us-east-2    | Ohio, USA                | US\_EAST\_2    |
| us-west-1    | Northern California, USA | US\_WEST\_1    |
| sa-east-1    | Sao Paulo, Brazil        | SA\_EAST\_1    |**Asia-pacific:**Asia Pacific| AWS Region     | Location               | Atlas Region     |
| -------------- | ---------------------- | ---------------- |
| ap-southeast-1 | Singapore              | AP\_SOUTHEAST\_1 |
| ap-southeast-2 | Sydney, NSW, Australia | AP\_SOUTHEAST\_2 |
| ap-south-1     | Mumbai, India          | AP\_SOUTH\_1     |
| ap-northeast-1 | Tokyo, Japan           | AP\_NORTHEAST\_1 |
| ap-northeast-2 | Seoul, South Korea     | AP\_NORTHEAST\_2 |
| ap-northeast-3 | Osaka, Japan           | AP\_NORTHEAST\_3 |**Europe:**Europe| AWS Region   | Location            | Atlas Region   |
| ------------ | ------------------- | -------------- |
| eu-west-1    | Ireland             | EU\_WEST\_1    |
| eu-central-1 | Frankfurt, Germany  | EU\_CENTRAL\_1 |
| eu-north-1   | Stockholm, Sweden   | EU\_NORTH\_1   |
| eu-west-2    | London, England, UK | EU\_WEST\_2    |

sections.heading: Available Search Tiers
sections.body:
Atlas provides various [Search Tier](select-tiers-for-search-nodes) for Search Nodes deployed on AWS (Amazon Web Services).The following table lists the search tiers available for each class of
hardware (low-CPU, high-CPU, storage-optimized), with default RAM and
storage measured in gibibytes (GiB):**Americas:**Americas**Low-cpu:**Low-CPU| Instance Size | Default RAM | Default Storage | vCPU |

sections.heading: Stream Processing Workspaces
sections.body:
> **Important:** To request an unlisted region, add your feedback
> to the [MongoDB Feedback Portal](https://feedback.mongodb.com/?category=7529546585152608284). Your
> input helps prioritize future expansions of Atlas Stream Processing coverage.Atlas supports deploying [Stream Processing Workspaces](atlas-sp-manage-spi) in the follow AWS (Amazon Web Services) regions:**Americas:**Americas| AWS Region   | Location               | Atlas Region   |
| ------------ | ---------------------- | -------------- |
| us-east-1    | Northern Virginia, USA | US\_EAST\_1    |
| us-east-2    | Ohio, USA              | US\_EAST\_2    |
| us-west-2    | Oregon, USA            | US\_WEST\_2    |
| ca-central-1 | Montreal, QC, Canada   | CA\_CENTRAL\_1 |
| sa-east-1    | Sao Paulo, Brazil      | SA\_EAST\_1    |**Asia-pacific:**Asia Pacific| AWS Region     | Location               | Atlas Region     |
| -------------- | ---------------------- | ---------------- |
| ap-southeast-1 | Singapore              | AP\_SOUTHEAST\_1 |
| ap-southeast-2 | Sydney, NSW, Australia | AP\_SOUTHEAST\_2 |
| ap-south-1     | Mumbai, India          | AP\_SOUTH\_1     |
| ap-northeast-1 | Tokyo, Japan           | AP\_NORTHEAST\_1 |**Europe:**Europe| AWS Region   | Location            | Atlas Region   |
| ------------ | ------------------- | -------------- |
| eu-west-1    | Ireland             | EU\_WEST\_1    |
| eu-central-1 | Frankfurt, Germany  | EU\_CENTRAL\_1 |
| eu-west-2    | London, England, UK | EU\_WEST\_2    |

sections.heading: Integrations
sections.body:
Along with [Amazon Web Services (AWS)](amazon-aws), the following
product integrations enable applications running on AWS (Amazon Web Services), such as
Amazon EC2, AWS Lambda, and Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS),
to use Atlas instances easily and securely.

sections.heading: Networking Services
sections.body:
- AWS PrivateLink: [private endpoint connections](private-endpoint)

- AWS Virtual Private Cloud (VPC): [VPC/VNet peering](vpc-peering)

sections.heading: Security and Identity Services
sections.body:
- AWS Identity Access Management (IAM) [Configure Database Users](mongodb-users)

- AWS Key Management Service (KMS):

  - [Encryption at Rest using Customer Key Management](security-kms-encryption)

  - [Configure client-side field level encryption with KMS](manual/core/csfle)

- AWS SSO: [Configure federated authentication to the MongoDB UI](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/apn/how-to-integrate-aws-single-sign-on-with-mongodb-atlas/)

sections.heading: Other AWS Services
sections.body:
- AWS CloudFormation: [Deploy and manage Atlas from CloudFormation](https://www.mongodb.com/blog/post/deploy-manage-mongodb-atlas-aws-cloud-formation)

- Amazon EventBridge: [Atlas Triggers integration with Amazon EventBridge](atlas-aws-eventbridge)

- Amazon Kinesis: [Send data to Atlas via Kinesis Data Firehose](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/big-data/integrating-the-mongodb-cloud-with-amazon-kinesis-data-firehose/)

- AWS S3: [Configure Atlas Data Federation to query data from S3](adf-configuration-file-aws)

sections.heading: Procurement
sections.body: - AWS Marketplace: [Pay for Atlas usage via AWS](https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/seller-profile?id=c9032c7b-70dd-459f-834f-c1e23cf3d092)

sections.heading: More Information
sections.body:
For more information on how to use AWS (Amazon Web Services) with Atlas most effectively,
review the following best practices, guides, and case studies:

- AWS App Runner: [Build Applications and APIs Faster with MongoDB Atlas and AWS App Runner](https://www.mongodb.com/blog/post/build-applications-and-apis-faster-with-mongodb-atlas-and-aws-app-runner)

- AWS Lambda: Best Practices Connecting from AWS Lambda
